channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Djibouti election set to extend Guelleh’s rule after constitutional changes
item.description: Djibouti’s President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh is expected to secure another term in the 10 April 2026 election after constitutional reforms removed the age limit for presidential candidates. The change, ap...

item.title: Young Nurse Builds Dream Home and Gets a Car at 23
item.description: Liyabona Socishe, a 23-year-old nurse, has stunned social media with her accomplishments. She shared her success on TikTok, showing off her fully furnished house in the village and a sleek car. The vi...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Continues Hearing Witnesses on Alleged Corruption in Tshwane
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, tasked with investigating claims of criminality, political interference, and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system, resumed proceedings on Friday...

item.title: Rivers Deputy Governor Calls for Transparency and Cooperation to Strengthen Health Service Delivery
item.description: Rivers State Deputy Governor, Professor Ngozi Odu, don talk say the improvement of health service for the state go depend on proper coordination, honesty, and joint effort between government agencies ...

item.title: AIADMK Candidate Experiences Health Scare During Campaign in Thiruvidaimaruthur
item.description: During the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections on Thursday, April 9, 2026, AIADMK candidate Ilamathi Subramanian, who previously served as MLA of Valangaiman, fainted while campaigning in the Thiruvidaimaru...

item.title: Brooklyn Beckham marks four years of marriage as family rift continues
item.description: Brooklyn Beckham, aged 27, has shared an emotional anniversary message with his wife, actress Nicola Peltz, celebrating four years of marriage while seemingly referencing his strained relationship wit...

item.title: Filipino Photojournalists Earn Recognition at 2026 World Press Photo Awards
item.description: Two Filipino photojournalists have been recognized in the prestigious 2026 World Press Photo contest, highlighting the country’s continued excellence in visual storytelling and documentary photography...

item.title: Royal Navy Monitors Russian Shadow Fleet Amid Legal Concerns Over Boarding Sanctioned Vessels
item.description: The Royal Navy has been unable to seize Russian 'shadow fleet' tankers passing through UK waters, despite authorisation from Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer for commando raids on sanctioned vessels. C...

item.title: How proposed CGT reforms may negatively impact young property investors
item.description: A proposed change to Australia's capital gains tax (CGT) could potentially hurt young Australians who have recently invested in properties. One such individual is Jawal Trivedi, a 25-year-old engineer...

item.title: Global temperatures and sea warming in March 2026 raise strong signs of El Niño’s return
item.description: March 2026 marked one of the hottest months on record worldwide, with average global surface temperatures measured at 1.48°C above pre-industrial levels, according to data from the Copernicus Climate ...

item.title: Around 500 Overseas Filipino Workers return from Abu Dhabi and Kuwait
item.description: Nearly 500 Filipinos have safely returned to the Philippines from Abu Dhabi and Kuwait, according to reports from ABS-CBN News. The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) has been actively coordinating t...

item.title: Police increase patrols to support vulnerable communities after attacks
item.description: The government has announced the deployment of additional specialist police patrols aimed at safeguarding vulnerable communities across the country. This decision follows a series of high-profile atta...

item.title: Kenya Power Employee Dies Following Assault Linked to Illegal Electricity Use in Isiolo
item.description: A Kenya Power worker tragically passed away after being attacked while on duty in Checheles, near Isiolo Town. The victim received emergency care locally and was later airlifted to Nairobi for special...

item.title: Ingram and Raptors Gear Up for Final Push to Secure Playoff Spot
item.description: The Toronto Raptors are focused on securing a spot in the NBA playoffs as the regular season comes to a close. Brandon Ingram, who has been a standout player for the team, emphasized the importance of...

item.title: Ferry Delays Spark Concern Among Halifax Business Community
item.description: Business leaders in Halifax are expressing significant concern over ongoing delays to the province’s ferry service. The disruptions are impacting commerce and tourism, particularly for communities rel...

item.title: President Ruto Emphasises Formal Dress Code for State House Events
item.description: During a State House event on April 10, 2026, President William Ruto publicly criticised Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja and other government officials for their inappropriate attire. Speaking at the ...

item.title: JD Vance Appointed to Lead US-Iran Ceasefire Negotiations in Islamabad
item.description: JD Vance, the US Vice President, is set to lead diplomatic talks aimed at turning the fragile Iran ceasefire into a sustainable peace agreement. At 41, Vance, who has largely stayed away from the spot...

item.title: Ukrainian Forces Report Shooting Down Drones Over the Middle East, According to Zelensky
item.description: According to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, Ukrainian military teams operating abroad successfully downed several drones in the Middle East amid the growing regional instability involving Isr...

item.title: Discovery of a 250-million-year-old embryo fossil reveals egg-laying habits of early mammal ancestors
item.description: Scientists have discovered the fossil of a 250-million-year-old embryo in South Africa, providing the first solid proof that ancient mammal ancestors, known as therapsids, laid eggs. The find, made ne...

item.title: South African Referees Tom and Siwela Selected for 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: South Africa will be proudly represented at the 2026 FIFA World Cup with the inclusion of referee Abongile Tom and assistant referee Zakhele Siwela in the final list of match officials. This marks a s...
